1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import {
23	  selectExercisesScaled,
24	  selectSectionsForVariation,
25	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
26	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
27	import {
28	  validateExerciseStructural,
29	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
30	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
31	  type StructuralFailure,
32	  type FailureCode,
33	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
34	import {
35	  validateExerciseSemantic,
36	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
37	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
38	import { RouterStrategy } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
39	
40	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
41	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
42	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
43	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
44	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
45	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
46	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
47	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
48	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
49	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
50	
51	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
52	export const STUCK_FAILURE_CODE = 'STUCK_AFTER_MAX_ATTEMPTS' as const
53	
54	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
55	
56	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
57	export interface StrategyResult {
58	  exerciseId: string
59	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
60	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
61	}
62	
63	/**
64	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
65	 *
66	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
67	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
68	 *
69	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
70	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
71	 * @param _payload    Payload instance (available for future AI strategy use)
72	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
73	 */
74	export async function runStrategy(
75	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
76	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
77	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
78	  payload: Payload,
79	<<<<<<< HEAD
80	  _exerciseIndex?: number,
81	): Promise<StrategyResult> {

85	  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload)
86	  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
87	
88	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
89	  return {
90	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
91	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',

97	/**
98	 * Cap source-exercise block count to match the structural validator's
99	 * `TOO_MANY_SECTIONS` ceiling (5). Pure: returns a NEW exercise object with
100	 * a trimmed `content.blocks` array when needed, otherwise the original.
101	 *
102	 * Picks 5 representative blocks from the source via `selectSectionsScaled` —
103	 * the same scaling-random bucketing the lesson-level selector uses. Preserves
104	 * source order, so the variation reads like a natural shorter version of the
105	 * original rather than a random shuffle.
106	 *
107	 * Rationale: without this, a 10-block source always failed validation
108	 * (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS is a blocking failure → exercise dropped from output).
109	 * Admins doing deep variations on real lessons would silently lose any
110	 * exercise that exceeded the block cap.
111	 */
112	export function trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise: ExerciseDoc): ExerciseDoc {
113	  const blocks = exercise.content?.blocks
114	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length <= 5) return exercise
115	  const picked = selectSectionsForVariation(blocks, 5)
116	  logger.info(
117	    { exerciseId: exercise.id, originalCount: blocks.length, trimmedTo: picked.length },
118	    'Source exercise has more than 5 blocks — trimming via variation-aware selector',
119	  )
120	  return {
121	    ...exercise,
122	    content: { ...exercise.content, blocks: picked },
123	  }
124	}
125	
126	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
127	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
128	  switch (code) {
129	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
130	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
131	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
132	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
133	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
134	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
135	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
136	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
137	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
138	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
139	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
140	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
141	      return 'regenerate'
142	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
143	      return 'regenerate'
144	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
145	      return 'skip'
146	    default:
147	      return 'skip'
148	  }
149	}
150	
151	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
152	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
153	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
154	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
155	// before deleting this assert.
156	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
157	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
158	void _concurrencyAssert
159	
160	/**
161	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
162	 *
163	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
164	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
165	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
166	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
167	 */
168	async function appendEntry(
169	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
170	  payload: Payload,
171	  duplicationId: string,
172	  exerciseRef: string,
173	  sectionIndex: number,
174	  code: string,
175	  message: string,
176	): Promise<void> {
177	  const action = suggestAction(code)
178	  try {
179	    const current = await payload.findByID({
180	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
181	      id: duplicationId,
182	      depth: 0,
183	      overrideAccess: true,
184	    })
185	    await payload.update({
186	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
187	      id: duplicationId,
188	      data: {
189	        [bucket]: [
190	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
191	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
192	          {
193	            exerciseRef,
194	            sectionIndex,
195	            code,
196	            message,
197	            suggestedAction: action,
198	            resolved: false,
199	          },
200	        ],
201	      } as never,
202	      overrideAccess: true,
203	    })
204	  } catch (err) {
205	    logger.error(
206	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
207	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
208	    )
209	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
210	  }
211	}
212	
213	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
214	export async function appendFailure(
215	  payload: Payload,
216	  duplicationId: string,
217	  exerciseRef: string,
218	  sectionIndex: number,
219	  code: string,
220	  message: string,
221	): Promise<void> {
222	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
223	}
224	
225	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
226	export async function appendWarning(
227	  payload: Payload,
228	  duplicationId: string,
229	  exerciseRef: string,
230	  sectionIndex: number,
231	  code: string,
232	  message: string,
233	): Promise<void> {
234	  return appendEntry('warnings', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
235	}
236	
237	/**
238	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
239	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
240	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
241	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
242	 */
243	async function appendOutputExercise(
244	  payload: Payload,
245	  duplicationId: string,
246	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
247	): Promise<void> {
248	  try {
249	    const current = await payload.findByID({
250	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
251	      id: duplicationId,
252	      depth: 0,
253	      overrideAccess: true,
254	    })
255	    const existing =
256	      (current as unknown as { outputExercises?: OutputExerciseMapping[] }).outputExercises ?? []
257	    await payload.update({
258	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
259	      id: duplicationId,
260	      data: { outputExercises: [...existing, mapping] } as never,
261	      overrideAccess: true,
262	    })
263	  } catch (err) {
264	    logger.error(
265	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef: mapping.sourceExerciseId },
266	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',
267	    )
268	  }
269	}
270	
271	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
272	export type ExerciseDoc = {
273	  id: string
274	  content?: { blocks?: ContentBlock[] }
275	}
276	
277	/** Mapping entry from source exercise to generated output exercise. */
278	export interface OutputExerciseMapping {
279	  sourceExerciseId: string
280	  outputExerciseId: string
281	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
282	}
283	
284	/** Failure entry stored on the LessonDuplications record. */
285	export interface FailureEntry {
286	  exerciseRef: string
287	  sectionIndex: number
288	  code: FailureCode | string
289	  message: string
290	  suggestedAction: 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep'
291	  resolved: boolean
292	}
293	
294	/**
295	 * Create the output lesson (draft) by deep-copying every source-lesson field.
296	 *
297	 * Earlier versions only copied title/slug/status/chapter/course/tenant/locale,
298	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
299	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
300	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
301	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
302	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
303	 */
304	async function createOutputLesson(
305	  payload: Payload,
306	  sourceLessonId: string,
307	  level: string,
308	): Promise<string> {
309	  const source = await payload.findByID({
310	    collection: 'lessons',
311	    id: sourceLessonId,
312	    depth: 0,
313	    overrideAccess: true,
314	  })
315	  const sourceData = source as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
316	  // Drop Payload-managed + lineage fields; the rest carries over.
317	  //   slug:           regenerated by formatSlugAsync from the new title.
318	  //   blocks:         references source exercise IDs; rebuilt by the exercise
319	  //                   auto-add hook as new variation exercises are saved.
320	  //   translatedFrom: this is a fresh variation, not a translation copy.
321	  //   createdBy:      set to the duplicating admin via createdByField hook.
322	  const {
323	    id: _id,
324	    createdAt: _c,
325	    updatedAt: _u,
326	    slug: _ignoreSlug,
327	    blocks: _ignoreBlocks,
328	    translatedFrom: _ignoreTranslatedFrom,
329	    createdBy: _ignoreCreatedBy,
330	    ...rest
331	  } = sourceData as Record<string, unknown> & {
332	    id?: unknown
333	    createdAt?: unknown
334	    updatedAt?: unknown
335	    slug?: unknown
336	    blocks?: unknown
337	    translatedFrom?: unknown
338	    createdBy?: unknown
339	  }
340	  void _id
341	  void _c
342	  void _u
343	  void _ignoreSlug
344	  void _ignoreBlocks
345	  void _ignoreTranslatedFrom
346	  void _ignoreCreatedBy
347	  const base = (rest.title as string) ?? 'Lesson'
348	  const newLesson = await payload.create({
349	    collection: 'lessons',
350	    data: {
351	      ...rest,
352	      title: `${base} - Variation (${level})`,
353	      status: 'draft', // never publish a duplicate by default
354	    } as never,
355	    overrideAccess: true,
356	  })
357	  return newLesson.id
358	}
359	
360	/**
361	 * Create a draft exercise in the output lesson with the generated blocks.
362	 * Returns the mapping entry for tracking.
363	 */
364	export async function createOutputExercise(
365	  payload: Payload,
366	  result: StrategyResult,
367	  outputLessonId: string,
368	): Promise<OutputExerciseMapping> {
369	  const ex = await payload.create({
370	    collection: 'exercises',
371	    data: {
372	      title: `Variation of ${result.exerciseId}`,
373	      lesson: outputLessonId,
374	      content: { blocks: result.blocks },
375	      status: 'draft',
376	    } as never,
377	    overrideAccess: true,
378	  })
379	  return {
380	    sourceExerciseId: result.exerciseId,
381	    outputExerciseId: ex.id,
382	    strategy: result.strategy,
383	  }
384	}
385	
386	/**
387	 * Process a single exercise through the duplication pipeline.
388	 *
389	 * Steps:
390	 *  1. Run strategy → get new exercise content
391	 *  2. Structural validation → collect failures
392	 *  3. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script' → semantic validation
393	 *  4. On any failure: stream failure to DB, return null
394	 *  5. On all pass: return StrategyResult for the orchestrator to use
395	 */
396	async function processExercise(
397	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
398	  duplicationId: string,
399	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
400	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
401	  payload: Payload,
402	): Promise<{
403	  result: StrategyResult | null
404	  tokensUsed: { inputTokens: number; outputTokens: number }
405	}> {
406	  const exerciseRef = exercise.id
407	
408	  // Pre-trim source blocks. The structural validator caps exercises at 5
409	  // blocks (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS — renderer / UX limit). Source exercises with
410	  // 9-10 blocks would always fail variation. We sub-sample to 5 using the
411	  // same scaling-random selector that picks exercises from a lesson: pick 1
412	  // block per evenly-spaced bucket across the source, seeded for
413	  // reproducibility. Preserves source order, so the variation feels like a
414	  // natural truncation of the original.
415	  const trimmedExercise = trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise)
416	
417	  // Step 1: Run strategy
418	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
419	  let tokensUsed = { inputTokens: 0, outputTokens: 0 }
420	  try {
421	    const strategyResponse = await runStrategy(trimmedExercise, level, subject, payload)
422	    strategyResult = strategyResponse
423	    tokensUsed = strategyResponse.tokensUsed
424	  } catch (err) {
425	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
426	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
427	    await appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
428	    return { result: null, tokensUsed }
429	  }
430	
431	  // Step 2: Structural validation. Split failures into blocking (drop the
432	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
433	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
434	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
435	  //
436	  // We pass the source exercise's blocks (post-trim) so the validator can
437	  // suppress MISSING_QUESTION when the source itself had an empty prompt
438	  // (e.g. legacy geometry exercises where the question is in the figure, not
439	  // as text). Otherwise every such variation would falsely fail.
440	  const sourceBlocks = (trimmedExercise.content?.blocks ?? []) as ContentBlock[]
441	  const structuralFailures: StructuralFailure[] = validateExerciseStructural(
442	    strategyResult.blocks,
443	    sourceBlocks,
444	  )
445	  const blockingFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
446	  const warningFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => !BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
447	
448	  // Record blocking failures and non-blocking warnings into separate buckets
449	  // so the review UI can show them under different headings.
450	  for (const failure of blockingFailures) {
451	    await appendFailure(
452	      payload,
453	      duplicationId,
454	      exerciseRef,
455	      failure.blockIndex ?? 0,
456	      failure.code,
457	      failure.message,
458	    )
459	  }
460	  for (const warning of warningFailures) {
461	    await appendWarning(
462	      payload,
463	      duplicationId,
464	      exerciseRef,
465	      warning.blockIndex ?? 0,
466	      warning.code,
467	      warning.message,
468	    )
469	  }
470	
471	  if (blockingFailures.length > 0) {
472	    return { result: null, tokensUsed }
473	  }
474	
475	  // Warning-only path: fill placeholders so the exercise renders. Warnings are
476	  // already recorded so the admin can find the TODOs in the review screen.
477	  if (warningFailures.length > 0) {
478	    strategyResult = {
479	      ...strategyResult,
480	      blocks: fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders(strategyResult.blocks),
481	    }
482	  }
483	
484	  // Step 3: Semantic validation. Skipped for:
485	  //  - level=none (deep clone, no AI variation to judge)
486	  //  - script strategy (deterministic, no hallucination risk)
487	  //  - warning-only path (we just inserted "_TODO: hint not provided by AI_"
488	  //    placeholders; a semantic reviewer would flag those as nonsensical and
489	  //    demote an exercise that should ship with a polish-later flag into a
490	  //    hard failure)
491	  if (strategyResult.strategy !== 'script' && level !== 'none' && warningFailures.length === 0) {
492	    const semanticResult = await validateExerciseSemantic(
493	      strategyResult.blocks,
494	      level,
495	      strategyResult.strategy,
496	      payload,
497	    )
498	    if (!semanticResult.ok) {
499	      await appendFailure(
500	        payload,
501	        duplicationId,
502	        exerciseRef,
503	        0,
504	        SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
505	        `Semantic mismatch: ${semanticResult.reasons.join('; ')}`,
506	      )
507	      return { result: null, tokensUsed }
508	    }
509	  }
510	
511	  return { result: strategyResult, tokensUsed }
512	}
513	
514	/**
515	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
516	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
517	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
518	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
519	 */
520	const FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS = 30_000
521	
522	/**
523	 * Default assumed worst-case time for one exercise (pass-1 + pass-2 +
524	 * validators on gemini-3.1-pro-preview). Used to decide whether the next
525	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
526	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
527	 */
528	const ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS = 300_000
529	
530	export type RunOutcome = 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' | 'failed' | 'in_progress'
531	
532	export interface RunOptions {

542	/**
543	 * Orchestrate the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record.
544	 * Resumable: skips exercises already processed in previous ticks and streams
545	 * each new output mapping to the DB as it completes. A Vercel function
546	 * timeout mid-run leaves a clean partial state — the next cron tick continues
547	 * from there.
548	 *
549	 * @param duplicationId  ID of the LessonDuplications record to process
550	 * @param payload        Payload instance
551	 * @param options.deadlineMs  Absolute timestamp by which to stop processing
552	 *
553	 * Pre-condition: record has status `pending` (fresh) or `running` (resuming).
554	 * Post-condition (returned outcome):
555	 *  - 'succeeded'   — all picked exercises produced an output, 0 failures
556	 *  - 'needs_review'— all picked exercises terminal, ≥1 failure
557	 *  - 'in_progress' — deadline hit, more work to do, status left as 'running'
558	 *  - 'failed'      — fatal error before any exercises processed
559	 */
560	export async function runDuplicationOrchestrator(
561	  duplicationId: string,
562	  payload: Payload,
563	  options: RunOptions = {},
564	): Promise<RunOutcome> {
565	  // Load the duplication record
566	  const duplication = await payload.findByID({
567	    collection: 'lesson-duplications',
568	    id: duplicationId,
569	    depth: 1, // need sourceLesson relationship
570	    overrideAccess: true,
571	  })
572	
573	  if (!duplication) {
574	    logger.error({ duplicationId }, 'LessonDuplications record not found')
575	    return 'failed'
576	  }
577	
578	  // Already terminal — nothing to do.
579	  if (
580	    duplication.status === 'succeeded' ||
581	    duplication.status === 'needs_review' ||
582	    duplication.status === 'failed'
583	  ) {
584	    logger.warn(
585	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
586	      'LessonDuplications record already terminal, skipping',
587	    )
588	    return duplication.status as RunOutcome
589	  }
590	
591	  // Accept both 'pending' (first tick) and 'running' (resuming) — anything
592	  // else is unexpected.
593	  if (duplication.status !== 'pending' && duplication.status !== 'running') {
594	    logger.warn(
595	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
596	      'LessonDuplications record in unexpected status, skipping',
597	    )
598	    return 'failed'
599	  }
600	
601	  try {
602	    const sourceLessonId =
603	      typeof duplication.sourceLesson === 'string'
604	        ? duplication.sourceLesson
605	        : (duplication.sourceLesson as { id?: string })?.id
606	
607	    if (!sourceLessonId) {
608	      throw new Error('sourceLesson relationship is missing or invalid')
609	    }
610	
611	    // First tick: create output lesson, flip to running. Resuming tick:
612	    // reuse the existing output lesson recorded on the record.
613	    let outputLessonId: string
614	    const existingOutputLessonRef = duplication.outputLesson as
615	      | string
616	      | { id?: string }
617	      | null
618	      | undefined
619	    const existingOutputLessonId =
620	      typeof existingOutputLessonRef === 'string'
621	        ? existingOutputLessonRef
622	        : existingOutputLessonRef?.id
623	
624	    if (duplication.status === 'pending') {
625	      outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
626	        payload,
627	        sourceLessonId,
628	        duplication.level ?? 'light',
629	      )
630	      await payload.update({
631	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
632	        id: duplicationId,
633	        data: { status: 'running', outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
634	        overrideAccess: true,
635	      })
636	    } else {
637	      // resuming
638	      if (!existingOutputLessonId) {
639	        // Shouldn't happen — running record without an outputLesson means a
640	        // previous tick crashed before the initial write. Treat as fresh.
641	        outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
642	          payload,
643	          sourceLessonId,
644	          duplication.level ?? 'light',
645	        )
646	        await payload.update({
647	          collection: 'lesson-duplications',
648	          id: duplicationId,
649	          data: { outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
650	          overrideAccess: true,
651	        })
652	      } else {
653	        outputLessonId = existingOutputLessonId
654	      }
655	    }
656	
657	    const allExercises = await getSourceExercisesForLesson(payload, sourceLessonId)
658	
659	    // No artificial cap — resumability handles long runs across multiple
660	    // cron ticks. selectExercisesScaled still bounds at 20 per the original
661	    // product spec (admin scaling-random across the whole lesson).
662	    const PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP = 20
663	    const selectedExercises = selectExercisesScaled(
664	      allExercises as ExerciseDoc[],
665	      PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP,
666	    )
667	
668	    // Filter out exercises already processed on prior ticks. Sources of truth:
669	    //  1. The record's `outputExercises` — explicitly recorded successes.
670	    //  2. The record's `failures` — terminal per-exercise failures.
671	    //  3. Output exercises already present in the output lesson itself, even
672	    //     if their mapping never made it back into `outputExercises`. This
673	    //     closes the crash-window between createOutputExercise (writes the
674	    //     exercise to Mongo) and appendOutputExercise (records the mapping):
675	    //     if the function dies between those two calls, the exercise is
676	    //     durably saved but the record doesn't know about it; without this
677	    //     check the next tick would re-process the source and create a
678	    //     duplicate. We identify the source by the deterministic title
679	    //     `Variation of <sourceExerciseId>` set in createOutputExercise.
680	    const existingOutputExercises = await payload.find({
681	      collection: 'exercises',
682	      where: { lesson: { equals: outputLessonId } },
683	      limit: 0,
684	      depth: 0,
685	      overrideAccess: true,
686	    })
687	    const titleSourceIds = new Set<string>()
688	    const orphanMappings: OutputExerciseMapping[] = []
689	    const knownMappingSources = new Set(
690	      ((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
691	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
692	      ),
693	    )
694	    for (const ex of existingOutputExercises.docs as Array<{
695	      id: string
696	      title?: string
697	    }>) {
698	      const match = typeof ex.title === 'string' ? /^Variation of (\S+)/.exec(ex.title) : null
699	      if (match) {
700	        titleSourceIds.add(match[1])
701	        // Recover the mapping for any orphan exercise (created but never
702	        // recorded). Next tick start, we'll reconcile by appending these
703	        // mappings so the record matches reality going forward.
704	        if (!knownMappingSources.has(match[1])) {
705	          orphanMappings.push({
706	            sourceExerciseId: match[1],
707	            outputExerciseId: ex.id,
708	            strategy: 'ai',
709	          })
710	        }
711	      }
712	    }
713	
714	    // Reconcile orphan mappings recovered from the output lesson so the
715	    // duplications record reflects reality before this tick continues.
716	    for (const orphan of orphanMappings) {
717	      await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, orphan)
718	      logger.warn(
719	        { duplicationId, sourceExerciseId: orphan.sourceExerciseId },
720	        '[orchestrator] Recovered orphan output exercise (crash-window mapping)',
721	      )
722	    }
723	
724	    const processedIds = new Set<string>([
725	      ...((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
726	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
727	      ),
728	      ...((duplication.failures as unknown as Array<{ exerciseRef: string }>) ?? []).map(
729	        (f) => f.exerciseRef,
730	      ),
731	      ...titleSourceIds,
732	    ])
733	    const remaining = selectedExercises.filter((ex) => !processedIds.has(ex.id))
734	
735	    logger.info(
736	      {
737	        duplicationId,
738	        totalPicked: selectedExercises.length,
739	        alreadyProcessed: selectedExercises.length - remaining.length,
740	        remaining: remaining.length,
741	      },
742	      'Orchestrator tick start',
743	    )
744	
745	    const duplicationLevel = duplication.level as 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep'
746	    const duplicationSubject =
747	      (duplication.subject as DuplicationSubject | null | undefined) ?? 'mixed'
748	
749	    // Process exercises one at a time (CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 — appendEntry's
750	    // read-modify-write isn't safe under parallel writes; see compile-time
751	    // guard). Check deadline between exercises and bail out if the next one
752	    // can't fit, returning `in_progress` so the next tick continues.
753	    for (const exercise of remaining) {
754	      if (options.deadlineMs !== undefined) {
755	        const remainingMs = options.deadlineMs - Date.now()
756	        if (remainingMs < FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS + ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS) {
757	          logger.info(
758	            { duplicationId, remainingMs, remainingExercises: remaining.length },
759	            'Orchestrator tick yielding before deadline — leaving record running',
760	          )
761	          return 'in_progress'
762	        }
763	      }
764	
765	      const { result } = await processExercise(
766	        exercise,
767	        duplicationId,
768	        duplicationLevel,
769	        duplicationSubject,
770	        payload,
771	      )
772	
773	      if (result === null) continue
774	
775	      try {
776	        const mapping = await createOutputExercise(payload, result, outputLessonId)
777	        // Stream the success immediately so a function timeout doesn't lose
778	        // this exercise — the actual exercise doc is already in MongoDB
779	        // (createOutputExercise just created it), this just keeps the
780	        // duplications record in sync.
781	        await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, mapping)
782	      } catch (err) {
783	        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown create error'
784	        logger.error(
785	          { exerciseRef: exercise.id, err },
786	          'createOutputExercise failed — exercise dropped from output lesson',
787	        )
788	        await appendFailure(
789	          payload,
790	          duplicationId,
791	          exercise.id,
792	          0,
793	          GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE,
794	          `Failed to save variation: ${message}`,
795	        )
796	      }
797	    }
798	
799	    // All selected exercises terminal: finalize status. Re-read failures
800	    // count from the record since we streamed entries during the run.
801	    const finalRecord = await payload.findByID({
802	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
803	      id: duplicationId,
804	      depth: 0,
805	      overrideAccess: true,
806	    })
807	    const finalFailures = (finalRecord.failures as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
808	    const finalOutputs =
809	      (finalRecord.outputExercises as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
810	    const finalStatus: 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' =
811	      finalFailures === 0 ? 'succeeded' : 'needs_review'
812	
813	    logger.info(
814	      {
815	        duplicationId,
816	        total: selectedExercises.length,
817	        succeeded: finalOutputs,
818	        failed: finalFailures,
819	        finalStatus,
820	      },
821	      'Duplication orchestrator completed',
822	    )
823	
824	    await payload.update({
825	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
826	      id: duplicationId,
827	      data: {
828	        status: finalStatus,
829	      } as never,
830	      overrideAccess: true,
831	    })
832	    return finalStatus
833	  } catch (err) {
834	    logger.error({ duplicationId, err }, 'Orchestrator run failed')
835	    await payload.update({
836	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
837	      id: duplicationId,
838	      data: { status: 'failed' },
839	      overrideAccess: true,
840	    })
841	    throw err
842	  }
843	}
